title: Rent-to-Own Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate how much monthly rent credits count toward your property purchase with our free lease option calculator. Estimate total rent paid & remaining balance.
intro: A lease option (rent-to-own) allows you to rent a property while securing the right to purchase it at a set price within a timeframe, typically 1-3 years. A percentage of your monthly rent is credited toward the eventual purchase. This calculator helps you understand how rent credits accumulate and how much you'll need to pay at closing. Values and guidance are current as of 2026.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Monthly Rent × Credit Percentage = Monthly Rent Credit**. Direct calculation of how much rent counts toward your purchase price each month.
useCases: Understanding lease option agreements and terms

fields.label: Monthly Rent Credit %
fields.help: Percentage of monthly rent counted toward purchase (typically 20-30%).

explanation:
## What is a Lease Option?

A lease option (often called "rent-to-own") is a rental agreement that includes the right to purchase the property at a predetermined price within a specified timeframe. A percentage of your monthly rent is credited toward the purchase price, reducing the amount you need to pay when exercising the option.

## How the Calculation Works

This calculator applies the standard lease option formula: **Monthly Rent × Credit Percentage = Monthly Rent Credit**. Multiplying by the number of months gives you total accumulated credits. The calculator validates inputs against typical ranges and flags unusual values.

## Important Considerations

Lease option terms vary significantly by state and locality. Property taxes, insurance, and maintenance responsibility should be clearly defined in your agreement. This calculation is for reference only. For financial, legal, or tax decisions, consult with a qualified real estate attorney or financial advisor. Information is current as of 2026.

faq.a: A lease option is a rental agreement that includes the right (but not obligation) to purchase the property at a predetermined price within a specific timeframe, typically 1-3 years.

faq.q: How much of my rent counts toward the purchase?
faq.a: The percentage is negotiable in your lease agreement. Common rates range from 20-30% of monthly rent, though this varies based on market conditions and negotiations.
An upfront fee (typically 2-5% of the property value) paid by the tenant to secure the purchase option and right of first refusal.

faq.q: Can I terminate a lease option early?
faq.a: Yes, but early termination typically requires paying a penalty specified in your lease option agreement. The consequence for backing out varies by contract.

faq.q: What happens if I can't get financing to buy?
faq.a: If you're unable to secure a mortgage by the option deadline, you generally lose the property and any forfeited option fee. Consult your agreement for specifics.

faq.q: How is a lease option different from rent-to-own?
faq.a: Lease options give you the right (optional) to buy. Rent-to-own agreements typically obligate you to purchase and may penalize you for not completing the purchase.

faq.q: Who pays property taxes and insurance?
faq.a: This depends on your specific agreement. Typically the property owner remains responsible, but confirm in writing before signing.
Consult a tax professional or accountant about your specific situation, as tax treatment depends on your agreement structure and local tax laws.
